note: CITED published external baselines shown side-by-side with our numbers in a release (tempdoc 623 T-3 / T-X). These are immutable citations, NOT projections of our runs — pinned by source + version. All values are nDCG@10. CAVEATS baked in per the T-X research: MIRACL baselines are DEV split (our axis is test; MIRACL test is held out for the WSDM'23 Cup), so a side-by-side must footnote dev≠test; German is NOT a canonical MIRACL language (surprise language) so its only published numbers come from BGE-M3's own non-canonical reproduction; BGE-M3 has NO published BEIR/SciFact number (omitted, not guessed). `self_reproduced:false` everywhere — none of these were re-run by us.

beir/scifact.version: Brewing BEIR (SIGIR 2024) Table 3 / SPLADE++ paper Table 4
beir/scifact.caveat: multifield indexing; matches our lexical 0.661 (tempdoc 623 U5). BM25-flat is 0.679. COMPARISON CLASS: single-model retriever — our 0.757 is a full hybrid (BM25+dense+SPLADE)+rerank pipeline, so this is a system-vs-component comparison; hybrid+rerank is expected to exceed single models.
SelfDistil variant = 0.702. COMPARISON CLASS: single-model learned-sparse retriever — our 0.757 is a full hybrid+rerank pipeline (system vs component).
confirmed independently in SPLADE++ and Brewing BEIR tables. COMPARISON CLASS: single-model late-interaction retriever — our 0.757 is a full hybrid+rerank pipeline (system vs component).

mixed/miracl-fr-2k.caveat: DEV split (≠ our test axis); do not mix with BGE-M3's BM25 (fr=0.458, different impl)

mixed/miracl-de-2k.caveat: NON-CANONICAL: German is a MIRACL surprise language with NO baseline row in the MIRACL paper; this is BGE-M3's own reproduction. DEV split. Flag as resting on a single non-canonical source.

mixed/legal-clerc-200.caveat: SCALE MISMATCH, not a comparison class issue: CLERC's own retrieval collection is 1.84M documents / 23.7M passages; our mixed/legal-clerc-200 corpus is a 198-document candidate pool (200 queries, following tempdoc 666's construction: one positive + curated negatives per query, mirroring CLERC's own query+positive+20-negatives record shape). nDCG@10 on a ~200-document pool is structurally far easier than on a 1.84M-document collection, so our 0.516 is NOT 'beating published BM25 (0.054)' — it reflects a much smaller candidate set. Cited to establish CLERC as a real, citable academic legal-retrieval benchmark grounding this corpus, not as a head-to-head score comparison.

Same scale-mismatch caveat as the BM25 row above — full 1.84M-doc/23.7M-passage collection vs. our 198-document pool. Listed as the strongest zero-shot dense baseline CLERC reports (ahead of E5 v2 0.0367, BGE En v1.5 0.0343, DPR 0.0224).
